meta.source: Microsoft Learn (learn.microsoft.com/microsoftteams) — portal paths, setting labels, and defaults grounded via the Microsoft Learn MCP on 2026-07-03.
generalNotes: This is one bundled meeting policy applied to the Global (Org-wide default) policy in Teams Admin Center > Meetings > Meeting policies — edit that policy rather than creating a new one, unless you're scoping it to a subset of users.
Changes to meeting policies can take up to 24 hours to take effect.

Several of these settings require Teams Premium (watermarks, copy/forward restriction, enforced participant-rename lockdown) and Copilot requires a Copilot license — they're flagged inline. Without those licenses, configure what you can and note the rest as N/A for the tenant.
'Anonymous users can join a meeting' also depends on the org-wide toggle at Meetings > Meeting settings > Participants — the per-policy setting only takes effect while the org-wide one is On.

requiredSettings.label: Meeting join & lobby — Who can bypass the lobby
requiredSettings.value: People who were invited (everyone else waits in the lobby until admitted)

requiredSettings.label: Meeting join & lobby — Anonymous users can join a meeting
requiredSettings.value: On (requires the org-wide toggle at Meetings > Meeting settings to also be On)

requiredSettings.label: Meeting join & lobby — Anonymous users and dial-in callers can start a meeting
requiredSettings.value: Off (anonymous/dial-in wait in the lobby until a verified participant starts the meeting)

requiredSettings.label: Recording & transcription — Meeting recording
requiredSettings.value: On — recordings save to OneDrive/SharePoint (Teams has no local-disk recording; this is the 'cloud only' behavior)

requiredSettings.label: Recording & transcription — Default expiration time
requiredSettings.value: 120 days (1–99,999; 'Recordings automatically expire' must be On)

requiredSettings.label: Recording & transcription — Copilot
requiredSettings.value: On only with retained transcript (requires a Copilot license)

requiredSettings.label: Meeting engagement — External meeting chat
requiredSettings.value: Off (users can't read/write chat in meetings hosted by untrusted external orgs)

requiredSettings.label: Content sharing — Watermark for shared content & video ⚠️ Teams Premium
requiredSettings.value: On for both screen-share content and camera video

requiredSettings.label: Meeting chat copy/forward restriction ⚠️ Teams Premium
requiredSettings.value: On (prevents copying/forwarding of meeting chat, live captions, and transcript)

requiredSettings.label: Participants can change their display name ⚠️ Teams Premium
requiredSettings.value: Off (participants can't rename themselves in the meeting)

steps: In the Teams admin center (admin.teams.microsoft.com), expand Meetings → Meeting policies, then open the Global (Org-wide default) policy (or the policy you're scoping).

Meeting join & lobby: set Who can bypass the lobby = People who were invited; Anonymous users can join a meeting = On; Anonymous users and dial-in callers can start a meeting = Off. (Confirm the org-wide Meetings > Meeting settings > Anonymous users can join a meeting is also On.)
Recording & transcription: Meeting recording = On; Recordings automatically expire = On; Default expiration time = 120; Copilot = On only with retained transcript.
Meeting engagement: External meeting chat = Off.
Content sharing (Teams Premium): turn on the watermark for both shared content and video.
Teams Premium items: enable the meeting-chat copy/forward restriction, and set participants can change their display name = Off. Skip these if the tenant has no Teams Premium licenses.
Save. Allow up to 24 hours to propagate.

Note: users will see recordings expire after 120 days, watermarks on shared screen/video, external guests may now land in the lobby, no self-rename, and local-disk recording gone (OneDrive/SharePoint only) — brief them in advance.
